Dashboard Archive: 2026-03-09 Home
About This Dashboard
What It Does
Long-term momentum ranker scoring 700+ tickers on returns from 1 month to 10 years, with relative performance vs SPY over 1y/5y/10y. Same scoring engine as the short-term ranker but optimized for longer investment horizons and secular trends.
Today's Reading
# Market Analysis: momentum-ranker-long Today's data shows two distinctly different momentum profiles ranking at the top. CLMT (#1, score 91.5) displays explosive near-term gains with a 143% one-year return and exceptional 595% five-year performance, though it has underperformed SPY by 34% over the past decade, suggesting this is a relatively recent breakout. SGOL (#2) presents a more balanced profile with consistent gains across all timeframes—301% over 10 years, 191% over 5 years—and notably passes all five momentum ratio thresholds (versus CLMT's three passes), indicating sustained acceleration across multiple periods. The key contrast is that CLMT's extreme outperformance versus SPY (126% over 1y, 522% over 5y) reversed dramatically into underperformance over longer horizons, while SGOL maintains positive relative returns even at the 10-year mark (55% vs SPY), suggesting more structural strength. Both stocks sit above all major moving averages (SMA 30/50/100/200), but CLMT's "bad_spy_score" of 0.7675 flags heightened risk relative to SPY correlation, whereas SGOL's lower score of 0.3491 indicates more defensive characteristics.
